publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2024-12-13 23:59:35 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection contains documentation from various sources (NGOs, government agencies, project brochures, publications, and reports) covering reconciliation efforts in Cambodia in the post-Khmer Rouge era gathered by Laura McGrew during her PhD research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Laura McGrew Cambodia collection, #8895. Rare and Manuscript Holdings of the Division of Asia Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Laura McGrew is a consultant in the fields of human rights, reconciliation, and transitional justice in Cambodia (where she's worked since 1987) as well as Rwanda, Sri Lanka, Afghanistan, Nepal, and Bosnia. In addition to graduate degrees in medical science from Emory University and public policy from Johns Hopkins University's School of Advanced International Studies, McGrew received her PhD from Coventry University in 2011 upon the completion of her dissertation, "Reconciliation in Cambodia: victims and perpetrators living together, apart."

did.unittitle: Asia Pacific Center for Justice and Peace
Cambodia: the Royal Government on Trial

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paper by McGrew and Frederick C. Brown.
